commands.repair_reason: Human follow-up is needed for refreshed real behavior proof and pending CI rather than an automated repair, because I found no discrete current-head code blocker.; Cleared: Cleared: the diff changes rollback mechanics around existing npm install security validation without adding dependencies, workflows, permissions, secrets handling, or new code-execution surfaces. commands.review_summary:
The branch adds managed npm root snapshot/restore rollback for npm-backed plugin install failures, regression coverage and a changelog entry, plus small provider-registry and session-key adjustments.

Reproducibility: yes. I did not run tests in this read-only review, but source inspection shows current main still uses destructive rollback after validation failures, and the PR body documents a targeted current-main regression that fails when a blocked update removes the previous plugin.
commands.review_followup: Human follow-up is needed for refreshed real behavior proof and pending CI rather than an automated repair, because I found no discrete current-head code blocker.
